checklist.entry: Separate MCP host, client and server responsibilities.
Treat MCP as a protocol layer for connecting applications to external context and tools, not as application business logic itself.
Model server-provided features as resources, prompts and tools.
Model client-provided features as sampling, roots and elicitation where supported.
Use JSON-RPC message discipline for protocol interactions.
Respect lifecycle management, initialization and capability negotiation before using optional features.
Record supported capabilities and unsupported feature paths.
Do not assume tools, resources, prompts, sampling, roots or elicitation exist unless declared and negotiated.
